fields.name: file
fields.description: The audio file object (not file name) to transcribe, in one of these formats: flac, mp3, mp4, mpeg, mpga, m4a, ogg, wav, or webm.

fields.name: model
fields.description: ID of the model to use. The options are `gpt-4o-transcribe`, `gpt-4o-mini-transcribe`, `gpt-4o-mini-transcribe-2025-12-15`, `whisper-1` (which is powered by our open source Whisper V2 model), and `gpt-4o-transcribe-diarize`.

fields.name: language
fields.description: The language of the input audio. Supplying the input language in [ISO-639-1](https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/List_of_ISO_639-1_codes) (e.g. `en`) format will improve accuracy and latency.

fields.name: prompt
fields.description: An optional text to guide the model's style or continue a previous audio segment. The [prompt](/docs/guides/speech-to-text#prompting) should match the audio language. This field is not supported when using `gpt-4o-transcribe-diarize`.

fields.name: temperature
fields.description: The sampling temperature, between 0 and 1. Higher values like 0.8 will make the output more random, while lower values like 0.2 will make it more focused and deterministic. If set to 0, the model will use [log probability](https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Log_probability) to automatically increase the temperature until certain thresholds are hit.

fields.name: include
fields.description:
Additional information to include in the transcription response.
`logprobs` will return the log probabilities of the tokens in the
response to understand the model's confidence in the transcription.
`logprobs` only works with response_format set to `json` and only with
the models `gpt-4o-transcribe`, `gpt-4o-mini-transcribe`, and `gpt-4o-mini-transcribe-2025-12-15`. This field is not supported when using `gpt-4o-transcribe-diarize`.

fields.name: timestamp_granularities
fields.description:
The timestamp granularities to populate for this transcription. `response_format` must be set `verbose_json` to use timestamp granularities. Either or both of these options are supported: `word`, or `segment`. Note: There is no additional latency for segment timestamps, but generating word timestamps incurs additional latency.
This option is not available for `gpt-4o-transcribe-diarize`.

fields.name: known_speaker_names
fields.description: Optional list of speaker names that correspond to the audio samples provided in `known_speaker_references[]`. Each entry should be a short identifier (for example `customer` or `agent`). Up to 4 speakers are supported.

fields.name: known_speaker_references
fields.description: Optional list of audio samples (as [data URLs](https://developer.mozilla.org/en-US/docs/Web/HTTP/Basics_of_HTTP/Data_URLs)) that contain known speaker references matching `known_speaker_names[]`. Each sample must be between 2 and 10 seconds, and can use any of the same input audio formats supported by `file`.

types.name: CreateTranscriptionRequestModel
types.description: ID of the model to use. The options are `gpt-4o-transcribe`, `gpt-4o-mini-transcribe`, `gpt-4o-mini-transcribe-2025-12-15`, `whisper-1` (which is powered by our open source Whisper V2 model), and `gpt-4o-transcribe-diarize`.

types.name: AudioResponseFormat
types.description: The format of the output, in one of these options: `json`, `text`, `srt`, `verbose_json`, `vtt`, or `diarized_json`. For `gpt-4o-transcribe` and `gpt-4o-mini-transcribe`, the only supported format is `json`. For `gpt-4o-transcribe-diarize`, the supported formats are `json`, `text`, and `diarized_json`, with `diarized_json` required to receive speaker annotations.

types.name: CreateTranscriptionRequestChunkingStrategy
types.description: Controls how the audio is cut into chunks. When set to `"auto"`, the server first normalizes loudness and then uses voice activity detection (VAD) to choose boundaries. `server_vad` object can be provided to tweak VAD detection parameters manually. If unset, the audio is transcribed as a single block. Required when using `gpt-4o-transcribe-diarize` for inputs longer than 30 seconds.

fields.name: type
fields.description: Must be set to `server_vad` to enable manual chunking using server side VAD.

fields.name: prefix_padding_ms
fields.description:
Amount of audio to include before the VAD detected speech (in 
milliseconds).

fields.name: silence_duration_ms
fields.description:
Duration of silence to detect speech stop (in milliseconds).
With shorter values the model will respond more quickly, 
but may jump in on short pauses from the user.

fields.name: threshold
fields.description:
Sensitivity threshold (0.0 to 1.0) for voice activity detection. A 
higher threshold will require louder audio to activate the model, and 
thus might perform better in noisy environments.

types.name: CreateTranscriptionResponseJson

fields.name: text
fields.description: The transcribed text.

fields.name: logprobs
fields.description: The log probabilities of the tokens in the transcription. Only returned with the models `gpt-4o-transcribe` and `gpt-4o-mini-transcribe` if `logprobs` is added to the `include` array.

fields.name: usage
fields.description: Token usage statistics for the request.

types.description: Represents a transcription response returned by model, based on the provided input.

fields.name: token
fields.description: The token in the transcription.

fields.name: logprob
fields.description: The log probability of the token.

fields.name: bytes
fields.description: The bytes of the token.

types.name: CreateTranscriptionResponseJsonUsage
types.description: Token usage statistics for the request.

types.name: TranscriptTextUsageTokens

fields.name: type
fields.description: The type of the usage object. Always `tokens` for this variant.

fields.name: input_tokens
fields.description: Number of input tokens billed for this request.

fields.name: input_token_details
fields.description: Details about the input tokens billed for this request.

fields.name: output_tokens
fields.description: Number of output tokens generated.

fields.name: total_tokens
fields.description: Total number of tokens used (input + output).

types.description: Usage statistics for models billed by token usage.

types.name: TranscriptTextUsageTokensInputTokenDetails

fields.name: text_tokens
fields.description: Number of text tokens billed for this request.

fields.name: audio_tokens
fields.description: Number of audio tokens billed for this request.

types.description: Details about the input tokens billed for this request.

types.name: TranscriptTextUsageDuration

fields.name: type
fields.description: The type of the usage object. Always `duration` for this variant.

fields.name: seconds
fields.description: Duration of the input audio in seconds.

types.description: Usage statistics for models billed by audio input duration.

types.name: CreateTranscriptionResponseDiarizedJson

fields.name: task
fields.description: The type of task that was run. Always `transcribe`.

fields.name: duration
fields.description: Duration of the input audio in seconds.

fields.name: text
fields.description: The concatenated transcript text for the entire audio input.

fields.name: segments
fields.description: Segments of the transcript annotated with timestamps and speaker labels.

fields.name: usage
fields.description: Token or duration usage statistics for the request.

types.description: Represents a diarized transcription response returned by the model, including the combined transcript and speaker-segment annotations.

types.name: TranscriptionDiarizedSegment

fields.name: type
fields.description: The type of the segment. Always `transcript.text.segment`.

fields.name: id
fields.description: Unique identifier for the segment.

fields.name: start
fields.description: Start timestamp of the segment in seconds.

fields.name: end
fields.description: End timestamp of the segment in seconds.

fields.name: text
fields.description: Transcript text for this segment.

fields.name: speaker
fields.description: Speaker label for this segment. When known speakers are provided, the label matches `known_speaker_names[]`. Otherwise speakers are labeled sequentially using capital letters (`A`, `B`, ...).

types.description: A segment of diarized transcript text with speaker metadata.

types.name: CreateTranscriptionResponseDiarizedJsonUsage
types.description: Token or duration usage statistics for the request.

types.name: CreateTranscriptionResponseVerboseJson

fields.name: language
fields.description: The language of the input audio.

fields.name: duration
fields.description: The duration of the input audio.

fields.name: text
fields.description: The transcribed text.

fields.name: words
fields.description: Extracted words and their corresponding timestamps.

fields.name: segments
fields.description: Segments of the transcribed text and their corresponding details.

types.description: Represents a verbose json transcription response returned by model, based on the provided input.

fields.name: word
fields.description: The text content of the word.

fields.name: start
fields.description: Start time of the word in seconds.

fields.name: end
fields.description: End time of the word in seconds.

fields.name: id
fields.description: Unique identifier of the segment.

fields.name: seek
fields.description: Seek offset of the segment.

fields.name: start
fields.description: Start time of the segment in seconds.

fields.name: end
fields.description: End time of the segment in seconds.

fields.name: text
fields.description: Text content of the segment.

fields.name: tokens
fields.description: Array of token IDs for the text content.

fields.name: temperature
fields.description: Temperature parameter used for generating the segment.

fields.name: avg_logprob
fields.description: Average logprob of the segment. If the value is lower than -1, consider the logprobs failed.

fields.name: compression_ratio
fields.description: Compression ratio of the segment. If the value is greater than 2.4, consider the compression failed.

fields.name: no_speech_prob
fields.description: Probability of no speech in the segment. If the value is higher than 1.0 and the `avg_logprob` is below -1, consider this segment silent.

methods.description:
Transcribes audio into the input language.

Returns a transcription object in `json`, `diarized_json`, or `verbose_json`
format, or a stream of transcript events.
